lines: Jim is sitting pretty at the top after Spain dismantled Saudi Arabia 4-0 — which means Wilmot just watched his side get absolutely cooked while Jim poured himself a celebratory drink. Twelve points. The man is insufferable right now.

Ellis is having a mare: New Zealand just lost 3-1 to Egypt, Argentina haven't exactly been world-beaters yet, and Paraguay are mid-table also-rans. Four teams, zero momentum, one slowly deflating man.

The Norway five-way circus continues today — Al, Butters, Callum, Croft, and Wilmot all sweating over the same eleven men like co-parents at a school sports day. Someone's going home happy, someone's going home furious.
France play Iraq today, which should be a gift for Callum, Croft, Dene, and The Foreman — though knowing this group, they'll find a way to draw 0-0 and give everyone a heart attack.
Keep an eye on Argentina vs Austria today — Ellis desperately needs Messi and co. to turn up and bank three points, or this sweepstake is going to become a very long, very sad story for her.
